Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
We're partnering with a large, forward-thinking organisation to find a Lead AI Engineer who will play a pivotal role in shaping and delivering enterprise-wide AI capability. This is a high-impact leadership role where you'll drive innovation, influence strategy, and deliver real business outcomes through cutting-edge AI solutions.

As the Lead AI Engineer, you'll lead the design, development, and deployment of AI and machine learning solutions at scale. You'll work closely with senior stakeholders, architects, and data teams to embed AI into core business operations, driving smarter decision-making, automation, and performance improvements.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy and hands-on engineering, giving you the opportunity to shape AI direction while still building and delivering solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Design and deploy end-to-end AI/ML solutions that deliver measurable business value
Lead the adoption of modern AI frameworks, including LLMs, NLP, and generative AI
Build and scale AI solutions using Azure, Databricks, MLflow, and Delta Lake
Establish and manage MLOps pipelines for model deployment, monitoring, and optimisation
Develop reusable AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain, LlamaIndex) to accelerate delivery
Ensure AI solutions meet governance, ethical, and compliance standards
Partner with stakeholders to align AI initiatives with strategic objectives
Mentor and lead engineers and data scientists, fostering a high-performing AI capability


Key skills & experience

Strong background in Python and/or TypeScript with 8+ years in software/data engineering
Proven experience delivering ML and deep learning solutions (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn)
Expertise in MLOps, CI/CD, and model lifecycle management
Hands-on experience with Azure cloud and Databricks ecosystem
Exposure to LLMs, generative AI, and frameworks like LangChain or similar
Strong data engineering capability (Spark, PySpark, Delta Lake)
Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and Infrastructure-as-Code
Demonstrated ability to lead teams and influence stakeholders


If you're ready to step into a leadership role where you can shape the future of AI within a large organisation, apply now or reach out to Ali••••••••••@talentinternational.com for further information.

Senior ML Engineer role for global company

Your new company

HAYS have partnered with a global company in their search for a Senior Software Developer with deep Python and Machine Learning expertise to join a high-performing consulting team delivering cutting-edge software solutions into a large-scale industrial environment.

Your new role

You'll be working on mission-critical platforms that support highly automated, electrified operations, focusing on real-time decisioning, optimisation, and intelligent system orchestration. These platforms integrate advanced software engineering with production-grade machine learning, driving efficiency, reliability, and performance in a complex operational setting.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of backend engineering and applied machine learning, and who is passionate about building resilient, scalable systems where ML is central to business outcomes.

What you'll need to succeed


Strong, hands-on Python development experience in production environments
Proven experience deploying and integrating machine learning models into real-world systems
Solid understanding of ML lifecycle, including training, validation, deployment, and monitoring
Experience working with computationally intensive ML workloads and optimising performance

Technical Expertise

8+ years' experience in software development, with a focus on backend systems
Deep knowledge of distributed systems architecture
Experience with microservices, Docker, and Kubernetes
Familiarity with event/message-driven systems (Kafka, RabbitMQ, MQTT)
Strong Linux systems experience (e.g. Ubuntu, RedHat)
Experience with CI/CD and IaC tools (e.g. GitHub Actions, Terraform)
Proficiency in at least one additional language (C++, C#, Rust, etc.) advantageous

Desirable Experience

Background in real-time, high-availability environments highly regarded
Experience mentoring engineers or leading technical delivery
Strong system design and problem-solving capabilities



What you'll get in return

Work on cutting-edge machine learning applications in production environments
Be part of a high-impact transformation programme
Opportunity to influence architecture, ML strategy, and engineering standards
Collaborate with cross-functional experts in a technically challenging domain


What you need to do now


If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV, or call us now.

If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion on your career.

Job Title: Technical Lead - Agentic AI - $180k + Super + Bonus - Sydney
Ad Text:
Technical Lead - Agentic AI (Microsoft Stack)
Permanent | Sydney | $180k + Super + Bonus 

An opportunity to join a high-visibility, greenfield Agentic AI program in a large Australian enterprise. 

This role is firmly embedded in a Microsoft-first environment, leveraging Azure AI capabilities to design and build next-generation, agent-based systems in a real-world enterprise setting.

The Opportunity You’ll be one of the early hires in a newly formed team, working alongside a recently appointed Agentic AI Engineer to define how intelligent systems are built and scaled on the Microsoft ecosystem.

This is a purely technical leadership role - not people management focused - where you’ll operate above senior engineer level, balancing hands-on development with architectural ownership.

~60% hands-on engineering
~40% architecture, system design, and technical direction
You’ll have the autonomy to shape framework decisions, while working within a Microsoft-aligned cloud and AI stack.

What You’ll Be Doing
Design and build agent-based AI systems using Azure AI services
Work extensively with Azure OpenAI, Cognitive Services, and Semantic Kernel
Define architecture, patterns, and engineering standards for Agentic AI solutions
Evaluate and evolve frameworks (including integration with tools like LangChain/LangGraph where appropriate)
Deliver production-grade AI systems within a Microsoft cloud-native environment
Influence the technical direction of a high-profile AI initiative
What We’re Looking For Core experience:

Strong software engineering background (Python preferred)
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure (essential)
Proven experience building with Azure OpenAI / Microsoft AI stack
Experience designing and deploying production-grade AI systems
Strong understanding of APIs, microservices, and cloud-native architecture within Azure
Highly regarded:

Experience with Semantic Kernel, Copilot stack, or Microsoft AI ecosystem tools
Exposure to agent frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph)
Background in consulting, product engineering, or innovation-led environments
Why This Role?
Work on cutting-edge Agentic AI use cases within a Microsoft ecosystem
Join at the ground level of a greenfield build
High visibility with direct alignment to senior leadership
Influence tooling, frameworks, and long-term architecture
Strong investment in Azure AI capability and innovation

Job Title: Principal Engineer - 225k to 300k Base + Bonus + Equity
Ad Text:
Permanent position paying salaries between $240k to $300k base + bonus + super
Market leader in AI across Australia, embedding intelligent systems into core platform architecture, engineering workflows and customer-facing products
Lead technical direction across a scaled engineering division, working closely with senior leadership to align engineering and AI strategy with business outcomes
Principal technical expert, shaping critical engineering decisions and influencing complex system design across one of Australia’s most advanced, high-traffic platforms
Drive innovation and delivery of large-scale platform solutions, providing technical leadership to a 200+ person engineering group while mentoring senior engineers and setting the standard for engineering excellence
Principal Engineer

This is one of the most influential Principal Engineer opportunities in Australia today. You will set the technical direction for one of the country’s most advanced engineering divisions and play a pivotal role in evolving a modern, customer-scale digital platform with over 50 million users.

I’m partnering with a market-leading organisation that is quietly building one of the most sophisticated software platforms in the country. The company is already profitable, well-funded and established, but it still runs with the pace and ambition of a high-growth product company. Their platform sits at the centre of their digital business, and they’re now looking for a Principal Engineer to drive engineering excellence and alignment across product and platform teams as they scale into the next chapter of growth.

If you're someone who thrives on solving engineering challenges at scale in fast-paced, product-led SaaS environments, this role gives you real influence where it counts, without the red tape. You bring the ability to zoom out and shape long-term technical strategy, while also zooming in to solve deep engineering challenges with clarity and pragmatism. You will report directly to the C-suite and operate within high-trust environments where technical depth, leadership, and clear communication are paramount. You will influence Engineering Managers, other Principal Engineers, and senior engineers, setting the standard for engineering culture across large, modern organisations.

Nice to have:

Ability to translate complex technical concepts into business outcomes and influence at the executive level
Experience mentoring and developing senior engineering leaders and fostering a high-performance culture
You bring clarity to complexity: able to break down ambiguous, high-scale problems into clear, executable strategies
Comfort working in fast-paced, ambiguous environments with a bias for innovation and pragmatic decision-making

Reach out to Luke on 04•• ••• 993, email luk•@preactarecruitment.com
or click 'Apply.

Who we are:

Milwaukee Tools is an industry leader in the manufacturing of cordless & electric power tools, accessories, and hand tools. We pride ourselves on an unrelenting strategic focus on powerful brands, innovative products and exceptional people. Our culture is centred around improving the lives of our users, something that not only drives our product development but underpins everything we do.

We are committed to ensuring our people have a great experience at Milwaukee, so in addition to an exciting and challenging role we also provide a range of great employee benefits including:

Lifestyle: Hybrid working model.
Discounts & Perks: Generous discounts on company products, and novated leasing available.
Personal Development: Access to professional development programs.
Health & Wellbeing: Paid Parental Leave and Employee Assistance Program.
Social: Recognition programs and social events to celebrate our team's achievements.

We're seeking a Research & Market Intelligence Analyst to join our Milwaukee‑based team and support data‑driven business decisions across the organization. In this role, you'll apply a strong understanding of forecasting techniques, financial reporting, commercial performance metrics, and economic indicators to analyse market dynamics and inform strategic planning.

You'll synthesize insights from internal performance data, government statistics and regulatory datasets, census information, and industry research, translating complex analyses into clear narratives and recommendations for executive and senior stakeholder audiences. Your work will play a key role in informing market planning, investment decisions, and long‑term growth strategy.

What your day-today look like:

Build, maintain, and refine forecasting models to support business planning by validating assumptions, reconciling data sources, and clearly documenting methodologies.
Conduct market sizing analyses (TAM, SAM, SOM) to quantify market opportunities to support strategic and commercial decisions by integrating internal performance data with external market, economic, and regulatory datasets.
Source, interpret, and analyse government and regulatory data across Australia and New Zealand, including datasets from ABS, Stats NZ, and ASIC, to support forecasting, market sizing, and strategic analysis.
Explore the use of AI-enabled approaches to support forecasting, scenario analysis, pattern identification and large-scale synthesis, whilst clearly documenting assumptions and limitations.
Develop, maintain, and integrate analytical databases into Qlik Sense dashboards using SQL for data extraction and transformation and Python for data preparation, ensuring accurate data models, reliable refresh processes, and reporting.
Identify opportunities to use AI tools to automate or streamline repetitive analysis, data preparation and reporting tasks, improving speed, consistency and insight turnaround.

What you will bring to the role:

Working knowledge of Python/ SQL and hands on experience designing, developing, and maintaining analytical databases and dashboards to support reporting and forecasting.
Solid understanding of forecasting techniques and experience interpreting financial reports, commercial metrics, and economic indicators.
Interest in and experience with applying AI tools to research, analytics or insight generation, with an ability to critically assess and validate AI generated outputs.
A highly organized self-starter with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Ability to think laterally to help identify alternate options and develop innovative solutions.

If you fit the above attributes and are excited at the prospect of joining a large, successful, and dynamic organisation, then please click APPLY NOW!

Integration Engineer

Salary: $140,000 package (including super)
Employment Type: Full-time
Location: Sydney (Hybrid Working)

Position Purpose

The Integration Engineer is responsible for designing, developing, and supporting robust integration solutions that enable seamless interoperability between systems, applications, and data sources across the organisation.

Working closely with cross-functional teams, this role ensures accurate, secure, and near real-time data exchange, contributing to scalable and high-performing digital platforms.

About the Environment

You will join a large, complex enterprise technology environment supporting core business operations, digital services, and research capabilities.

The technology function operates using Agile and SAFe methodologies, with squads aligned to platforms and products. This role plays a key part in delivering integration capabilities that enable enterprise-wide connectivity and innovation.

You'll work collaboratively within cross-functional teams, actively contributing to agile ceremonies such as sprint planning, stand-ups, and retrospectives, while helping foster a high-performing and delivery-focused culture.

Reporting Line
Reports to: Chapter Lead - Integration
Dotted line to: Platform Operations Manager
No direct reports
Key Responsibilities
Design, develop, and implement integration solutions across multiple systems and platforms
Build and maintain APIs, event-driven integrations, and data pipelines
Configure and support middleware and integration platforms
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and real-time synchronisation across systems
Perform testing, validation, and troubleshooting of integration solutions
Monitor and optimise integration performance and reliability
Maintain clear and detailed technical documentation
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to understand integration requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose solutions
Engage with external vendors and technology partners where required
Ensure all solutions align with cybersecurity and data privacy standards
Provide support for production issues and participate in on-call or after-hours activities when required
Continuously improve integration patterns, standards, and practices
Key Challenges
Integrating across complex and diverse technology ecosystems
Ensuring scalability and performance as data volumes grow
Maintaining security and compliance across interconnected systems
Balancing stakeholder needs with technical constraints and delivery timelines
Skills & Experience Required
Relevant tertiary qualification or equivalent practical experience
Proven experience delivering integration solutions in enterprise environments
Strong hands-on experience with integration tools and platforms (e.g. Boomi AtomSphere)
Experience with AWS services, including APIs, microservices, and data streaming
Solid understanding of API design, middleware, and integration patterns
Experience working within Agile (Scrum/Kanban) environments
Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving capability
Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Understanding of DevSecOps practices and secure integration design
Experience ensuring data integrity, performance, and reliability across systems
Nice to Have
Experience in large, complex or higher education environments
Exposure to event-driven architecture and modern integration patterns
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and automation
Behavioural Capabilities
Collaborative and team-oriented
Adaptable and responsive to change
Strong sense of ownership and accountability
Client-focused with a solutions mindset
Continuous learner with a proactive approach to improvement
Additional Requirements
Participation in after-hours work or on-call roster may be required
Commitment to workplace health and safety practices
Adherence to organisational policies, procedures, and code of conduct

Job Title: Lead Software Engineer - AI Platform Services
Ad Text:
Company Description

About SEEK

At SEEK, we serve a noble purpose: to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and to help organisations succeed.

By joining us, you’ll be part of a multi-national technology business that is far-reaching with a start-up working culture that focuses on a set of collaborative values and appreciates dynamic cultures. SEEK is a place where potential meets possibility – it’s where your career aspiration and our purpose can make great things happen.

Why join us?

Be part of a multi-national tech company with strong core values to help us solve complex challenges for our customers while building a flexible, exciting career – one that could take you anywhere.

We’re looking for people who thrive in dynamic teams and enjoy tackling regular new challenges in a large-scale, modern, and agile environment. As one of Asia Pacific’s largest tech companies, you’ll contribute to multi-lingual systems, solving high-impact problems to the benefit of millions of people within the region.

Be part of a fast-growing company with a strong culture and clear purpose, innovation and strong investment in responsible AI. SEEK offers you the opportunity to be involved in impactful work and be part of a great team. Expect a great investment in your career development with access to workshops, conferences and courses while you work alongside seasoned experts.

Job Description

About the role

You will lead the engineering function within the All the Ads team, part of SEEK's Pricing and Performance group. You will design and deliver AI/ML systems, data assets, and pipelines that help SEEK better understand hirers and job ads at scale. You will collaborate with data scientists and AI product managers across three problem domains, managing a team of engineers while driving technical excellence and rapid delivery. You will own system architecture, mature ML-Ops practices, and ensure smooth integration with partner teams across the business. Success means building high-performing cross-functional teams, meeting delivery roadmaps, and advancing AI capabilities that directly impact millions of users.

Key Responsibilities

Lead cross-functional team to apply latest AI/ML practices
Design system architecture that meets team objectives and SEEK standards
Provide technical leadership and mature ML-Ops practices
Foster rapid delivery culture; align team to roadmaps and timelines
Manage, coach, and grow engineers through line management
Collaborate with data science, product, and business stakeholders on key decisions
Oversee integration of backend services with partner teams
Qualifications

Skills and Experience

10+ years backend software engineering with strong technical skills
Proficient in Python and/or Java (required)
Strong experience with AWS cloud platform (required)
Strong SQL skills (required)
Experience with Spark/Databricks and ML engineering (desirable)
Proven delivery and people management experience
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Frequent events including sports days, year-end party, hackathon, and trivia

 

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us. Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those with eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Commercian Implementation & Service Engineer - Australia Full-Time | Field-Based | Nationwide & International Travel

About Dexory

Dexory is redefining how the logistics industry operates. Our autonomous robots and real-time data platform give warehouse operators unprecedented visibility into their operations; tracking, measuring and locating goods at speed and scale, while building a live digital twin of every facility we work in.

Backed by some of Europe's leading VCs and working with major players in global logistics, we're in a period of rapid growth. We're now expanding our presence in Australia, and we're looking for someone exceptional to lead this charge on the ground.

The Role

This is a dual-function position designed for someone who thrives in the field. You'll begin as our primary Deployment Engineer in Australia — owning end-to-end installation and commissioning of Dexory's robotic systems at client warehouses — then evolve into an ongoing Service Engineer role, maintaining and repairing the fleet you've helped build out.

⚠️ Travel requirement: This role is approximately 100% field-based across Australia, with occasional international travel. A valid Australian driver's licence and unrestricted passport are essential.

Because of the travel side of the role, we are not able to consider those on work visas currently.

What You'll Do

Deployment

Be the primary customer contact for installation and commissioning of Dexory's robotic platforms at warehouse sites across Australia

Plan and coordinate site visits, incorporating mechanical, electrical, software, and network components

Execute on-site system setup, calibration, configuration, and testing in line with project specifications

Develop and maintain clear installation, operational, and troubleshooting documentation

Work closely with commercial and engineering teams to ensure seamless handover from sale to live deployment

Service & Maintenance

Perform on-site servicing, fault-finding, and repairs for Dexory robots across your client base

Develop and implement service plans aligned with engineering recommendations

Maintain product traceability records and ensure compliance with quality assurance processes

Respond to field issues proactively to minimise client downtime

Feed real-world learnings back to engineering and product teams

What We're Looking For

Essential:

Hands-on background with autonomous hardware, robotics, computer vision, or LiDAR-based systems - this is highly valued

5+ years of electromechanical assembly, servicing and repair from a precision industry (aerospace, automotive, motorsport, railway, or industrial automation)

Comfortable diagnosing and resolving technical issues on customer premises, often under time pressure

Strong communicator; able to translate complex technical information clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences

Genuinely comfortable with a role that is predominantly field-based across Australia and occasionally international

Valid Australian driver's licence and unrestricted passport

Strong electrical and mechanical diagnostic skills with impeccable attention to detail

Desirable:

Linux operating system experience

Familiarity with Jira, Confluence, Slack, or G-Suite

Root cause analysis methodologies

Background in autonomous systems or computer vision applications

Why Join Dexory?

You'll be Dexory's face in Australia - the person clients trust to deliver and maintain technology that genuinely transforms how their operations run. Hands-on, high-impact, and genuinely varied work with a world-class engineering team behind you.

Own something real: build and maintain Dexory's entire Australian deployment footprint from the ground up

Career growth: as our Australian presence expands, so does the opportunity around you

Competitive salary aligned to Australian market rates

Personal development budget

25 days annual leave plus Australian public holidays

Superannuation contributions in line with Australian statutory requirements

High-performance, low-ego team culture

Our Operating Principles

Performance: high standards, outstanding results | Impact: big challenges, bigger results | Commitment: all in, every time | One Team: one mission, shared success

We value humility as much as talent. If you meet most — but not all — of what we're looking for, please apply. Diversity of background drives better engineering and better outcomes for our clients.

Dexory provides equal employment opportunities to all applicants without regard to race, colour, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability, or any other characteristic protected by law.
